title: Multistage builds do not get cached
body:
### Issue Description

If you have a Dockerfile with multiple steps that is being used by multiple services like this:

Repository: https://gitlab.com/chocolateimage/multistage

compose.yaml

```
services:
  service1:
    build:
      target: service1
      dockerfile: Dockerfile

  service2:
    build:
      target: service2
      dockerfile: Dockerfile

  service3:
    build:
      target: service3
      dockerfile: Dockerfile

  service4:
    build:
      target: service4
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
```

And the Dockerfile:

```Dockerfile
FROM alpine:latest AS base
WORKDIR /app

FROM base AS deps
COPY dependency-generator.sh /app/
RUN [ "./dependency-generator.sh" ]

FROM base AS service-base
COPY service-runner.sh /app

# Pretend all of the services below do different things

FROM service-base AS service1
COPY --from=deps /app/deps /app/deps
ENTRYPOINT [ "./service-runner.sh", "service1" ]

FROM service-base AS service2
COPY --from=deps /app/deps /app/deps
ENTRYPOINT [ "./service-runner.sh", "service2" ]

FROM service-base AS service3
COPY --from=deps /app/deps /app/deps
ENTRYPOINT [ "./service-runner.sh", "service3" ]

FROM service-base AS service4
COPY --from=deps /app/deps /app/deps
ENTRYPOINT [ "./service-runner.sh", "service4" ]
```

The `deps` stage will be executed multiple times, this does not happen with Docker itself. In this example it will not do much, but in a real setting where you would download stuff and do CPU intensive work, this can slow down the build process by a large amount.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `git clone https://gitlab.com/chocolateimage/multistage.git`
2. `podman-compose up`
3. The line `"Generating" your dependencies` gets printed multiple times


### Describe the results you received

The line `"Generating" your dependencies` from the `deps` stage gets printed multiple times.

### Describe the results you expected

I expected that just like in Docker, it would get built once, and the result will be used in the individual services.

### podman info output


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

In a fresh libvirt/QEMU machine with Arch Linux

### Additional information

I uploaded the example code on GitLab: https://gitlab.com/chocolateimage/multistage

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Hi @chocolateimage are you using inbuilt `podman compose` command or something to run this ?

> Hi [@chocolateimage](https://github.com/chocolateimage) are you using inbuilt `podman compose` command or something to run this ?

I am using `podman-compose build`, but `podman compose build` gives the same result.

I am using Podman Compose from this Arch package: https://archlinux.org/packages/extra/any/podman-compose/

I don't see `deps` building again on second build attempt, pasting logs of two consecutive builds. Perhaps try updating version again.

I am closing this since I am unable to reproduce this, but please comment below if you think bug still exists.

I tried with following version

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.6.0-dev

OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

@chocolateimage I think issue is you are using `podman-compose` instead of podman's inbuilt compose feature i.e `podman compose`.

`podman-compose` and `podman compose` are different.

> [@chocolateimage](https://github.com/chocolateimage) I think issue is you are using `podman-compose` instead of podman's inbuilt compose feature i.e `podman compose`.
> 
> `podman-compose` and `podman compose` are different.

`podman compose` calls `podman-compose` if it is installed, else it calls `docker-compose`
